key_insight: A modular active-learning pipeline combines interpretable linear meta-learning with adaptive uncertainty calibration to improve multi-objective molecular search under limited data.
engineering_relevance: The approach is useful for materials discovery workflows that need fast, transparent surrogate models to optimize competing chemistry objectives without the cost of full quantum evaluations.
method: Meta-train linear surrogate models across related chemical objectives and cheap auxiliary properties.
Embed the surrogates in an Efficient Global Optimization loop for multi-objective search.
Adaptively recalibrate confidence during the search to balance exploration and exploitation as candidates shift toward distributional tails.
evidence_signal: On a large-scale spin-crossover metal-organic complex search, the baseline was 78% worse in Pareto performance than the meta-learning approach, and dynamic confidence tuning outperformed over 50% of the statically calibrated front.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of auxiliary tasks and careful calibration, since uncertainty estimates in tail regions can still be brittle during active search.
paper_title: Interpretable Meta-Learning for Multi-Objective Chemical Search
